title: Discovery
summary: Discovery manages information exchange through mandatory disclosures, proportionality limits, and enforcement tools.
learningObjectives: Define the modern scope of discovery.
Recognize proportionality limits and work-product concerns.
Identify consequences for failing to disclose or obey discovery orders.

rules.title: Scope and Proportionality
rules.blackLetterLaw: Parties may obtain discovery regarding nonprivileged matter relevant to any party's claim or defense and proportional to the needs of the case.
rules.explanation: Discovery is broad but not limitless; proportionality cabins requests based on the stakes, burden, and access to information.
elements: Relevance to a claim or defense
Proportionality considering burden, importance, amount in controversy, and access

rules.title: Initial Disclosures
rules.blackLetterLaw: Absent exemption or stipulation, parties must provide initial disclosures identifying likely witnesses, relevant documents, damage computations, and insurance information.
rules.explanation: Initial disclosures front-load basic information without waiting for formal requests.
elements: Documents and electronically stored information the party may use
Insurance agreements that may satisfy all or part of a judgment
pitfalls: Waiting for interrogatories before disclosing core materials

rules.title: Sanctions and Enforcement
rules.blackLetterLaw: Courts may compel discovery and impose sanctions for disclosure failures, noncompliance with discovery orders, or spoliation under the applicable rules and standards.
rules.explanation: Sanctions range from fees to issue sanctions or dismissal, depending on the violation and prejudice.
elements: Motion to compel after good-faith effort when required
Potential fee shifting for unjustified resistance
Escalating sanctions for order violations or serious misconduct

questions.prompt: A party seeks a huge volume of marginally relevant records whose review would be extraordinarily expensive. What is the best modern limitation argument?

choices.text: Discovery must always be allowed if any relevance exists
The request fails because only admissible evidence is discoverable
The request is disproportionate to the needs of the case
The request is barred unless the records are public

questions.rationale: Modern discovery expressly requires proportionality, not just relevance.

questions.prompt: A party ignores its initial disclosure obligations and only produces likely witness names after a court order. Which response is most likely?

choices.text: No consequence, because disclosures are voluntary
Possible sanctions or fee shifting for noncompliance
Automatic dismissal with prejudice in every case

questions.rationale: Failure to make required disclosures can support sanctions, especially after noncompliance with a court order.
